comment:
QA pre-review of SDLC-FLOW-RS v0.1 (inaugural Flow State Requirements Specification under CR-111).

Procedural compliance verified:
- Frontmatter: title 'Flow State Requirements Specification' present and matches H1.
- Frontmatter: revision_summary present and meaningful, references authorizing CR-111.
- Required sections present: Purpose, Scope, Requirements, References.
- No placeholder content (no TBD/TODO/FIXME/{{}}).
- Scope arithmetic consistent: §2 declares 12 reqs across 5 groups (4+3+2+2+1); §3 contents match exactly.
- Requirement ID convention REQ-FS-{GROUP}-NNN applied consistently with no gaps or duplicates.
- References valid: CR-111 verified IN_EXECUTION; companion SDLC-FLOW-RTM, pattern reference SDLC-CQ-RS, and Quality Manual sections cited.
- Authorizing CR (CR-111) is in the correct state (IN_EXECUTION) for RS authoring under this CR.

Technical question flagged for TU review (not a QA finding — for independent TU judgment):
- REQ-FS-UI-002 specifies input layer order as 'System -> Modal -> Global -> HUD' with a parenthetical justifying Modal-before-Global. CLAUDE.md §6.2 documents the order as 'System -> Global -> Modal -> HUD'. tu_ui please confirm which ordering reflects the actual implementation in flow-state/ui/input_handler.py and whether the requirement text is correct.

Reviewers assigned: tu_scene (owns Scene/Command/Compiler architecture for ARCH and SIM-002), tu_sketch (CAD entities and solver for CAD reqs), tu_sim (particle engine for SIM-001), tu_ui (UI tree, OverlayProvider, input chain for UI reqs), bu (user-facing execution modes in APP-001). tu_input is not a registered QMS user; UI domain coverage of input handling assigned to tu_ui.

Recommend pre-review approval pending TU concurrence.

From a user-value perspective, the inaugural adoption RS is sound. The user-facing surfaces I care about are present and well-stated: three execution modes (Dashboard/Simulation/Builder) in REQ-FS-APP-001; the interaction servo (REQ-FS-CAD-003) which determines how dragging FEELS — pivoting, length-preservation, no rubber-banding; the runtime backend toggle (REQ-FS-CAD-002) which lets users self-tune performance; and the modal-before-global input ordering (REQ-FS-UI-002) which protects users from hotkey hijacking while typing in dialogs and explicitly addresses ghost-inputs after modals close. The qualitative-proof, inspection-only model (Section 1, mirroring SDLC-CQ) is a legitimate scoping choice for an Adoption RS bringing existing code into governance. One forward-looking observation (not blocking): subsequent granular CRs should add user-facing requirements not covered here — project save/load (persistence to disk), the user-facing Ctrl+Z/Ctrl+Y undo/redo contract (separate from the internal Command Pattern requirement), and any minimum responsiveness expectations for interactive dragging. These are implicitly relied on by users today but not yet committed in writing.

TU-UI domain review: The UI-relevant requirements (REQ-FS-ARCH-001 Air Gap, REQ-FS-ARCH-003 Scene/Session Split, REQ-FS-ARCH-004 ToolContext Facade, REQ-FS-UI-001 Hierarchical UI Tree with Overlays, REQ-FS-UI-002 Input Chain of Responsibility) accurately capture the architectural and behavioral invariants enforced in the current flow-state codebase. Verified: ToolContext (core/tool_context.py) exposes only command execution, view/geometry queries, and transient interaction state — never the full app. OverlayProvider protocol exists in ui/ui_widgets.py and is used by Dropdown; UIManager renders overlays after main tree. Input chain order in ui/input_handler.py matches RS: System -> Modal -> Global -> HUD, with the documented Modal-before-Global ordering so dialogs capture keyboard input before hotkeys. push_modal/pop_modal in app_controller.py both invoke reset_interaction_state on the root container, satisfying the anti-ghost-input clause. Note: focus management protocol (wants_focus / on_focus_lost) is an additional UI invariant not explicitly required here, but it is implicit in REQ-FS-ARCH-003's mention of focused element, and the RS's stated v1.0 scope (high-level invariants, not granular features) makes this acceptable for initial adoption. No defects identified in the UI domain scope.

TU-SCENE review: RECOMMEND.

Reviewed against the orchestration domain (core/scene.py, core/commands.py, core/command_base.py, core/tool_context.py, core/session.py, engine/compiler.py) and verified each requirement against the actual code at flow-state/main HEAD.

Verifications performed:
- REQ-FS-ARCH-001 (Air Gap): scene.execute() at core/scene.py:184 is the sole mutation entry; no UI-side sketch.entities.append/remove/pop calls exist (grep clean).
- REQ-FS-ARCH-002 (Command Pattern): core/command_base.py declares execute() and undo() as @abstractmethod and historize as an __init__ parameter (default True).
- REQ-FS-ARCH-003 (Scene/Session Split): Scene owns sketch, simulation, process_objects, commands (CommandQueue); Session owns mode, camera, selection, focused_element, current_tool. interaction_data correctly resides on Sketch (verified in model/sketch.py) and is not listed in Session, addressing the v0.2 correction.
- REQ-FS-ARCH-004 (ToolContext Facade): core/tool_context.py exposes only execute(), view queries, geometry queries, and transient state; the _app reference is private.
- REQ-FS-SIM-002 (Compiler Bridge): engine/compiler.py:236 sets is_static=3 (tethered) and engine/compiler.py:266 sets is_static=1 (static); rebuild() is invoked from Scene.update() step 1 on _topology_dirty, satisfying the 'when the Scene reports geometry has changed' clause.
- REQ-FS-UI-002 (Input Chain): ui/input_handler.py:115-129 confirms System -> Modals -> Global -> HUD order, matching the RS exactly.
- REQ-FS-APP-001 (Execution Modes): main.py:20 declares choices=['dashboard','sim','builder']; the requirement's hedge that flag values are an implementation detail is appropriate.

Minor observation (non-blocking): REQ-FS-CAD-001 says the Sketch supports lines and circles as 'primary geometric entities,' but model/geometry.py also defines a Point entity class (referenced in scene.py import paths and in handle registration for ProcessObjects). The word 'primary' is appropriately qualitative and the RS is explicitly high-level, so this does not warrant request-updates; future CRs that add granular CAD requirements may want to acknowledge Point entities explicitly.

The v0.3 revision_summary accurately reflects the changes since v0.2, and the document is internally consistent across Sections 2.2 and 3 on the 5-domain breakdown. The RS is fit for purpose as a lightweight v1.0 adoption baseline.

CAD/Geometry domain review (REQ-FS-CAD-001/002/003 and Compiler portion of REQ-FS-SIM-002): all claims verified against code. Lines/circles plus parallel/perpendicular/equal/length/radius/angle/coincident/collinear/midpoint constraints confirmed in model/constraints.py. PBD solver with runtime-switchable Python/Numba backend confirmed in model/solver.py via use_numba flag. Interaction Servo confirmed: mouse injected as interaction_data each frame in Solver.solve(). Compiler is_static=1 (static) / is_static=3 (tethered) values confirmed in engine/compiler.py. Compiler.rebuild() invoked on geometry change. Two minor non-blocking observations for future revisions: (1) REQ-FS-CAD-001's 'fixed' example is informal — it does not map to a single constraint class but informally evokes the entity 'anchored' flag plus FixedAngle/Length/Radius. The 'e.g.' softens this acceptably for a high-level RS; consider substituting 'length' or 'angle' in a future revision for crispness. (2) REQ-FS-SIM-002's parenthetical that the Compiler 'enables two-way CAD-Physics coupling' is technically correct (tethered atoms are the enabling mechanism for runtime spring feedback) but is in mild tension with the Compiler module docstring's 'Data flow is ONE-WAY' wording. The RS framing is more architecturally accurate; the module docstring is stale. No revision required. Lightweight high-level scope is appropriate per the stated v1.0 intent. Recommend.

TU-SIM domain review (REQ-FS-SIM-001 Particle Engine, REQ-FS-SIM-002 Compiler Bridge): RECOMMEND.

Verified each SIM requirement against actual code at flow-state/main HEAD.

REQ-FS-SIM-001 (Particle Engine):
- Flat NumPy arrays / DOD: confirmed at engine/simulation.py:51-69 (pos_x, pos_y, vel_x, vel_y, force_x, force_y, is_static, atom_sigma, atom_eps_sqrt, etc., all dtype=float32 / int32, capacity-sized).
- Verlet integration: confirmed in engine/physics_core.py:100-180. integrate_n_steps performs Velocity Verlet (position update with force/2m*dt^2 term, then half-step velocity update post-force-recompute).
- Spatial hashing: confirmed in engine/physics_core.py:49-97. build_neighbor_list uses a uniform-grid cell list (head/next_idx linked-list per cell with cell_size = r_list) for O(N) neighbor enumeration.

REQ-FS-SIM-002 (Compiler Bridge):
- is_static=1 for non-dynamic entities: confirmed at engine/compiler.py:266 in _add_static_atom (called when 'not w.dynamic' at compiler.py:169 and :211).
- is_static=3 for dynamic entities: confirmed at engine/compiler.py:236 in _add_tethered_atom (called when 'w.dynamic' at compiler.py:165 and :207).
- Two-way CAD<->Physics coupling: confirmed. Tether arrays (tether_entity_idx, tether_local_pos, tether_stiffness) plus apply_tether_forces_pbd in physics_core accumulate per-entity [fx, fy, torque] in entity_forces, providing force feedback from atoms to parent entities. The 'two-way' wording in the RS parenthetical is technically accurate; the stale 'Data flow is ONE-WAY' docstring at compiler.py:7 (already noted by tu_sketch) describes the static-only legacy and is superseded by the tether mechanism.
- Rebuild on geometry change: Compiler.rebuild() exists at compiler.py:55 and is invoked by Scene.update() on topology change (corroborated by tu_scene's verification).

Non-blocking observations (NOT grounds for request-updates):
1. REQ-FS-SIM-002 captures the topology-change rebuild path but does not mention the fast path (sync_entity_arrays / sync_static_atoms_to_geometry in simulation.py:358-508) used when geometry MOVES but topology is UNCHANGED. For a v1.0 high-level invariant this is acceptable: the rebuild is the bridge invariant; the move-only fast path is a performance optimization that preserves the same is_static=1/3 atom topology. Future granular SIM CRs may want to formalize the move-vs-rebuild contract.
2. REQ-FS-SIM-001 does not mention Numba JIT explicitly, even though every kernel in physics_core.py is @njit-decorated and Simulation._warmup_compiler() is run at startup. This asymmetry with REQ-FS-CAD-002 (which DOES require both Python and Numba backends for the solver) is intentional and acceptable: the simulation has only the Numba backend and Numba is an implementation choice for performance, not a logical invariant of the particle engine. No revision required.

The v1.0 lightweight scope is fit for purpose. The two SIM requirements faithfully capture the structural and behavioral character of the simulation domain as it actually exists in flow-state/. Recommend.

QA review of SDLC-FLOW-RS v1.1 (revision authored under CR-112 ToolContext Migration Completion).

PROCEDURAL COMPLIANCE - VERIFIED:
- Frontmatter title present: 'Flow State Requirements Specification' (matches H1 'SDLC-FLOW-RS: Flow State Requirements Specification').
- Frontmatter revision_summary present and substantively describes the changes (REQ-FS-ARCH-004 strengthening, REQ-FS-CAD-003 strengthening, scope of unchanged requirements).
- Required sections present: Purpose, Scope, Requirements, References. Section ordering and numbering preserved from v1.0.
- No placeholder content found (no TBD, TODO, FIXME, XXX, {{}}).
- Scope arithmetic intact: section 2 declares 12 reqs across 5 groups (4+3+2+2+1); section 3 contents match exactly. No requirement count change.
- Requirement ID convention REQ-FS-{GROUP}-NNN preserved; no gaps, no duplicates.
- Authorizing CR (CR-112) verified IN_EXECUTION; correctly added to References section.
- v1.0 reference retained alongside v2.0 in references (CR-111 still cited as the authorizing change record for the v1.0 baseline).

SCOPE INTEGRITY (per QMS-Policy section 6):
- CR-112 section 5.7 pre-approved touching REQ-FS-ARCH-004 and 'may need a wording adjustment' to REQ-FS-CAD-003.
- Diff against v1.0 confirms exactly REQ-FS-ARCH-004 and REQ-FS-CAD-003 are modified; the other 10 requirements are textually unchanged.
- No silent scope expansion.

SUBSTANTIVE CHANGES VERIFIED:
- REQ-FS-ARCH-004 v1.1 explicitly forbids direct application references in tools and explicitly extends the facade scope to include interaction-state mutation and ProcessObject registration.
- REQ-FS-CAD-003 v1.1 adds the facade-routed injection clause requiring ctx.set_interaction_data / update_interaction_target / clear_interaction_data rather than direct sketch.interaction_data mutation.
- Both changes are consistent with CR-112's stated objective of closing the Air Gap exception that CR-111's RTM v1.0 acknowledged.

FINDING - REVISION_SUMMARY VERSION MISMATCH:
The revision_summary opens with 'CR-112 v2.0: ...' but the QMS engine has assigned this revision version 1.1, not 2.0. The CR-112 plan stated 'Document version advances v1.0 to v2.0' but the engine increments minor versions for non-major revisions. The revision_summary will be archived with version 1.1 and the embedded 'v2.0' label will be permanently inconsistent with the version field. Two acceptable resolutions: (a) revise the revision_summary to drop or reword the 'v2.0' label so it reads consistently with the engine-assigned 1.1; or (b) if a major-version bump is intended, escalate to the orchestrator to determine whether engine versioning policy should be changed and re-route accordingly. Recommend (a) for minimal disruption — the substantive rationale in the revision_summary is otherwise sound and should be preserved.

REVIEWERS ASSIGNED for v1.1 cycle: tu_ui (ToolContext facade is consumed by UI tools; REQ-FS-ARCH-004 owner domain), tu_sketch (REQ-FS-CAD-003 governs Sketch.interaction_data injection into solver), tu_scene (Scene owns the Sketch and the Air Gap is a Scene-level architectural invariant). tu_sim and bu not assigned — no SIM domain change, no user-facing behavioral change.

VERDICT: REQUEST-UPDATES on the revision_summary version label only. The substantive REQ changes are correct and well-scoped; once the version label is reconciled, this revision is ready for TU concurrence and approval.

Second-cycle review. The prior finding (revision_summary opening with 'CR-112 v2.0:') has been correctly addressed — it now reads 'CR-112 v1.1:' and includes a transparent parenthetical disclosing the CR-112 textual inconsistency, with a commitment to address it via CR-112 execution comments. Good.

However, the same defect persists at a second location within this RS: Section 4 (References) still describes CR-112 as 'the authorizing change record for the v2.0 revision'. This phrasing characterizes THIS RS's own current revision as v2.0, when the engine-assigned version is v1.1. The revision_summary's parenthetical explicitly scopes the disclosure to the CR's textual inconsistency, not to internal text within the RS itself.

Requested correction: Section 4, second bullet, change 'the v2.0 revision' to 'the v1.1 revision' (or equivalent phrasing that matches the engine-assigned version of this document).

All other content verified:
- title: 'Flow State Requirements Specification' — present and correct
- revision_summary opens with 'CR-112 v1.1:' — correct
- Substantive requirement strengthening of REQ-FS-ARCH-004 and REQ-FS-CAD-003 is sound and matches CR-112 §2.2 scope
- Section 2 requirement count (12) matches the breakdown (4+3+2+2+1)
- All other 10 requirements unchanged from v1.0 — verified by comparison
- No TBD/TODO/FIXME placeholders found
- Document structure follows the v1.0 pattern consistently

TU reviewers tu_ui and tu_sketch have been assigned to review the substantive content of the strengthened requirements (ToolContext facade enforcement and Interaction Servo facade routing) in parallel.

QA third-cycle review: RECOMMEND. Frontmatter title='Flow State Requirements Specification' present. revision_summary present and accurately describes v1.1 scope (REQ-FS-ARCH-004 and REQ-FS-CAD-003 strengthened; other 10 requirements unchanged). Verified prior-cycle findings: the two body-text 'v2.0' references have been corrected to 'v1.1' (References §4: CR-111 now scoped to 'v1.0 baseline'; CR-112 referenced as 'authorizing change record for the v1.1 revision'). Remaining 'v2.0' mentions are confined to the revision_summary's parenthetical disclosure, which verbatim cites CR-112 §5.7/§5.8/§7.5 wording — cross-checked against CR-112-draft.md and confirmed accurate quotation. The disclosure transparently flags the CR-vs-engine inconsistency and commits to remediation in CR-112 execution comments; this is appropriate contemporaneous documentation, not a deficiency. Substantive REQ wording verified: REQ-FS-ARCH-004 explicitly forbids direct app reference and requires facade-routed Sketch.interaction_data mutations; REQ-FS-CAD-003 adds the facade-routed injection clause naming ctx.set_interaction_data / update_interaction_target / clear_interaction_data. All required sections present (Purpose, Scope, Requirements, References, END OF DOCUMENT marker). No placeholders (TBD/TODO/FIXME) found. No typos or formatting inconsistencies found. Independent judgment per QMS-Policy §2.2: the parenthetical disclosure is the correct way to handle the CR-vs-RS version-label discrepancy — suppressing it would obscure a real text-vs-engine inconsistency that downstream reviewers need to see. tu_ui and tu_scene assigned for domain review of REQ-FS-ARCH-004 and REQ-FS-CAD-003 respectively.

Independent technical review (TU-SCENE / Orchestration domain) of SDLC-FLOW-RS v1.1.

VERDICT: Recommend.

Scope: Two requirements touched (REQ-FS-ARCH-004, REQ-FS-CAD-003); other 10 unchanged.

FINDINGS:

1. REQ-FS-ARCH-004 (ToolContext Facade) is correctly strengthened. The v1.0 wording 'Tools shall receive a ToolContext...never the full application reference' was permissive about receipt without forbidding retention; the v1.1 language 'shall interact...exclusively through' and 'shall not hold a direct application reference (no self.app or equivalent passthrough)' closes that ambiguity precisely. The enumerated facade capabilities (command execution, view/geometry queries, transient interaction-state management including set_interaction_data / update_interaction_target / clear_interaction_data, ProcessObject registration) match the existing tool_context.py surface plus the add_process_object method CR-112 §5.4 will add. Coherent with CR-112's implementation plan.

2. REQ-FS-CAD-003 (Interaction Servo) is correctly extended. The added clause routing injection through ctx.set_interaction_data / update_interaction_target / clear_interaction_data rather than direct Sketch.interaction_data mutation is consistent with REQ-FS-ARCH-004 and matches the four target sites identified in tools.py (788, 950, 978, 1061) at the FLOW-STATE-1.0 baseline.

3. Air Gap scoping is correct. REQ-FS-ARCH-004 forbids *tools* from mutating outside the facade; it does NOT forbid Scene/solver from reading sketch.interaction_data directly. This properly preserves CR-112 §5.3's deliberate non-policed exception (Scene.update at scene.py:324 reads interaction_data directly — Scene owns the model and is not a tool). The Air Gap principle is about UI-layer mutation of model state; the revised wording respects that scope.

4. Minor wording tension (non-blocking): The phrase 'persistent model state — including the Sketch's interaction_data field' is slightly imprecise because interaction_data is technically a transient solver-input field that lives on the Sketch object, not 'persistent' in the sense of CLAUDE.md §4 ('the Document'). A more precise phrasing would be 'model-side state' or 'state owned by the model layer.' However, the operational rule (tools route through the facade) is unambiguous, the inclusion of interaction_data as a scope clarification is correct, and this does not affect implementation. Acceptable as-is.

5. The revision_summary's transparency about the CR-112 v1.0→v2.0 vs. engine-assigned v1.0→v1.1 versioning inconsistency is appropriate. The substantive scope is the same; noting this in CR-112 execution comments is the right resolution.

6. No impact on orchestration-layer invariants: Scene/Session split (REQ-FS-ARCH-003), Command Pattern (REQ-FS-ARCH-002), and base Air Gap (REQ-FS-ARCH-001) are unchanged. The update loop, command/undo semantics, and state ownership model remain intact.

7. Forward-ready: The RS as revised admits and constrains the planned CR-112 implementation correctly. Migrating the four interaction_data sites and adding has_interaction_data()/add_process_object() satisfy the strengthened text without further RS adjustment.

Recommendation: Approve.

RECOMMEND. Independent technical review per SOP-002 within the UI domain, third cycle (recovers from prior v2.0/v1.1 version-label drift).

Substantive scope verified: only REQ-FS-ARCH-004 (ToolContext Facade) and REQ-FS-CAD-003 (Interaction Servo) changed; all other 10 requirements byte-identical to v1.0. Engine-assigned v1.1 is the correct semver treatment for 'tighten wording, no requirement removed/replaced'; the CR-112 narrative's v2.0 references are textual errors flagged appropriately in the revision_summary.

REQ-FS-ARCH-004 (UI domain — primary):
- Correctly forbids direct application references in tools (no self.app or equivalent passthrough). Aligns with the 'no God Object' principle in CLAUDE.md §5.5.
- Enumerated facade operations (command execution, view/geometry queries, transient interaction-state management with the three named drag-injection methods, ProcessObject registration) match tool_context.py:427-458 today, with add_process_object scoped under CR-112 §5.4 — consistent with 'Proposed State' authoring per QMS-Policy.
- Crucially closes the Air Gap exception that v1.0 acknowledged: Sketch.interaction_data lives on the model (sketch.py:33), is therefore persistent state, and must flow through the facade. The new wording correctly recognizes this.

REQ-FS-CAD-003 (UI behavior in second sentence):
- The new clause requiring facade-routed injection (set_interaction_data / update_interaction_target / clear_interaction_data) closes a gap in v1.0 where the *what* was specified (mouse as constraint target) but not the *how* (which path tools use). Method names match the facade exactly.
- Mutually consistent with REQ-FS-ARCH-004's general rule.

Forward-looking framing is correct: the RS revision describes the post-CR-112 Proposed State; the RTM at the FLOW-STATE-1.1 qualified commit will provide verification evidence. This is the standard GMP 'specifications first, then verify' pattern.

Editorial: revision_summary is verbose but the version-drift transparency is appropriate and the substance is sound — not worth a request-updates cycle.

No blocking issues within the UI domain.

QA pre-approval review of SDLC-FLOW-RS v2.1 (cosmetic cycle authorized by CR-112).

VERIFICATION (with evidence):

Frontmatter:
- title: 'Flow State Requirements Specification' (present, non-empty)
- revision_summary: present, descriptive, ~110 words. References CR-112. Correctly characterizes the cycle as 'cosmetic v2.1 -> v3.0', identifies the substantive change as v1.0 -> v2.0 strengthening of REQ-FS-ARCH-004 and REQ-FS-CAD-003, and explains the prior revision_summary's misreading of engine versioning behavior (drafts increment minor; approvals bump major). Wording is internally consistent and matches qms status output (effective v2.0, draft v2.1).

Document structure:
- Sections present: 1 Purpose, 2 Scope, 3 Requirements (3.1 ARCH, 3.2 CAD, 3.3 SIM, 3.4 UI, 3.5 APP), 4 References. END OF DOCUMENT marker present.
- Scope count consistent: declared 12 reqs across 5 groups (4+3+2+2+1); counted 12 rows total.

Substantive content:
- All 12 requirement rows (REQ-FS-ARCH-001 through REQ-FS-APP-001) are byte-identical to v2.0 effective. No silent substantive changes piggybacked on this cosmetic cycle. Scope integrity per QMS-Policy section 6 is honored.

Section 4 References:
- CR-112 entry rewritten in parallel with the revision_summary correction: now reads 'authorizing change record for the strengthened REQ-FS-ARCH-004 and REQ-FS-CAD-003 (v1.0 -> v2.0 substantive revision; subsequent v2.0 -> v3.0 cycle was a revision_summary cosmetic correction with no substantive RS content change).' Consistent with the new revision_summary. Other reference entries unchanged.

Placeholder/error scan:
- No TBD, TODO, FIXME, XXX, or {{}} placeholders found.
- No typos found.
- Formatting consistent throughout (tables, horizontal rules, bolded requirement names).

Traceability:
- Authorizing CR-112 referenced. Parent CR-111 referenced. Adjacent SDLC docs (RTM, CQ-RS) and Quality-Manual sections referenced.

TU assignment judgment (QMS-Policy section 2.4):
- The change is purely cosmetic: a revision_summary rewording and a parallel reference rewording. No requirement text is modified. No Flow State domain (architecture, CAD, simulation, UI, application shell) is materially affected because the requirements governing those domains are unchanged. Per the Quality Unit Handbook ('Template/process changes may need no TUs. ... Use judgment.'), no TU domain expertise adds signal to this review. Zero TUs assigned.

VERDICT: All review criteria satisfied. Recommend approval.
